architecture.summary: An autoregressive Transformer model generates Renaissance counterpoint in MIDI symbolic format by flattening multi-voice polyphonic music into a sequence. The model uses tokenization of symbolic note events and incorporates a symbolic component that enforces contrapuntal rules as constraints during generation. Information flows from encoded token sequences through transformer layers to predicted next tokens, with symbolic rule enforcement applied to filter or guide valid outputs.

claims.statement: The proposed model incorporates a symbolic component that enforces fundamental contrapuntal rules.
Flattened sequence models, when combined with appropriate tokenization and symbolic constraints, are capable of generating rich, multi-layered contrapuntal music.
The research explores the capacity of deep learning models to manage independent voices and strict stylistic constraints.
The thesis compares multiple data representation strategies with distinct tokenization methods.
